name: Cce
description_short: CCE Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union established in 1958 in Fargo, North Dakota, serving over 1,400 members with $15.6M in assets.
description_long: CCE Federal Credit Union was established in 1958 and is headquartered in Fargo, North Dakota, where it operates as a state-chartered credit union. With over 65 years of history, it has built a cooperative financial institution focused on serving North Dakota residents. The credit union ranks as the 23rd largest in North Dakota and maintains a single branch location at 316 10th Street N in Fargo. CCE offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, wire transfers, and online bill pay. Members can access services through online banking, a mobile app available on iOS and Android, e-statements, and traditional lobby banking. The credit union emphasizes competitive rates and lower fees as benefits of its not-for-profit structure, which allows earnings to be returned to members rather than shareholders. As a not-for-profit credit union, CCE distinguishes itself through its cooperative membership model and commitment to serving the local Fargo community. It operates with over 3 dedicated employees and maintains direct customer service through phone contact at (701) 237-4493. The institution provides financial calculators, mortgage and auto loan tools, and a community Q&A forum to support member financial planning and decision-making. However, CCE operates with significant geographic and infrastructure limitations. With only 1 branch and 0 ATMs, it lacks the convenience network of larger institutions, making it impractical for members who travel frequently or need widespread access points. Its asset base of $15.6M is modest, and membership eligibility is restricted to North Dakota residents. The single branch location means all in-person services are limited to one physical site, which may present challenges for members outside Fargo.
pros: Not-for-profit structure offers better rates and lower fees than commercial banks
Offers full suite of products: checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, and personal loans
Mobile app available on both iOS and Android for convenient access
Online bill pay and e-statements for digital banking convenience
Established in 1958 with 65+ years of operational history and stability
Local community focus with dedicated customer service at (701) 237-4493
Financial calculators and tools available to help members with planning
cons: Only 1 branch location in Fargo, ND with zero ATMs limits accessibility
Membership restricted to North Dakota residents only
Asset base of $15.6M is relatively small, potentially limiting lending capacity
Single branch model creates a bottleneck for in-person services
No reviews currently available on the listing, making it difficult to assess member satisfaction
best_for: North Dakota residents living in or near Fargo seeking a local credit union alternative
Members prioritizing not-for-profit structure and community-focused banking over convenience
Customers comfortable with primarily digital banking via mobile app and online banking
Small business owners and individuals seeking personalized relationship banking
diagnosis: CCE Federal Credit Union is best suited for North Dakota residents, particularly those in the Fargo area, who value not-for-profit banking and are comfortable with limited physical branch access. The main caveat is its geographic restriction and single-branch model, which makes it impractical for members requiring multiple locations or nationwide ATM access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
